title: Review and Update Logged Events

checks.name: Advanced Audit Policy Subcategories are Configured
checks.purpose: Verify all relevant advanced audit policy subcategories (Account Logon, Account Management, Detailed Tracking, Logon/Logoff, Object Access, Policy Change, Privilege Use, System) are explicitly configured

checks.name: Audit Policy Covers Minimum Required Event Categories
checks.purpose: Confirm the current audit policy includes success and failure tracking for all CMMC-required event types

checks.name: Advanced Audit Policy Subcategories are Configured
checks.purpose: Verify all relevant advanced audit policy subcategories are explicitly configured via Group Policy or auditpol

checks.name: Audit Policy Covers Minimum Required Event Categories
checks.purpose: Confirm the current audit policy includes success and failure tracking for all CMMC-required event types

checks.name: GPO-Enforced Audit Policy is Applied
checks.purpose: Verify the audit policy is enforced via Group Policy Object and not subject to local override

checks.name: Audit Rules Cover Required Event Categories
checks.purpose: Verify /etc/audit/rules.d/ contains rules covering file access, user/group changes, privilege escalation, network configuration changes, and system calls

checks.name: Audit Rules are Immutable or Locked
checks.purpose: Confirm auditd rules include -e 2 (immutable) flag to prevent runtime modification without a reboot

checks.name: Audit Rules Cover Required Event Categories
checks.purpose: Verify /etc/audit/rules.d/ contains rules covering file access, user/group changes, privilege escalation, network configuration changes, and system calls

checks.name: Audit Rules are Immutable or Locked
checks.purpose: Confirm auditd rules include -e 2 (immutable) flag to prevent runtime modification without a reboot

remediation: Review and configure advanced audit policy subcategories via Group Policy (Windows) or /etc/audit/rules.d/ (Linux). Ensure all required CMMC event categories are covered with both success and failure tracking. Lock Linux audit rules with -e 2.
